camps.name: Camp Legacy – Regular Week
camps.description: 45-minute classes in diverse activities including robotics, cooking, sports, and arts. Located at Ladue Middle School, 9701 Conway Rd.
camps.note: $175 deposit secures spot · $15 off early deposit · $10 off per additional session

camps.name: Camp Legacy – Excursion Week
camps.description: Field trip-focused week visiting venues like water parks, skating rinks, and entertainment centers. Located at Ladue Middle School.

camps.name: Something Rotten Jr.
camps.description: Two-week musical theater intensive. Week 1 at Ignite Theatre Company, Week 2 at Robert E. Reim Theatre. Must enroll in both weeks.
camps.note: Verify 2026 dates with Ignite — pricing based on 2025 schedule

camps.name: Grade School Art Camp
camps.description: Hands-on exploration of silk-screening, pinhole photography, ceramics, drawing, painting, and fresco. Ends with a public art show on June 12.
camps.note: Must enroll both weeks · Verify 2026 dates

camps.name: Musical Theatre Camp I
camps.description: Broadway-focused training in vocals, choreography, and stagecraft. 9am–3pm. Ends with a family performance showcase.

camps.name: The Candy Lab I
camps.description: Candy-making paired with chemistry lessons on crystallization, solubility, and chemical reactions. 10:30am–12:30pm.

camps.name: Basketball Camp I
camps.description: Skill-building through drills, mini-games, and focused instruction for all ability levels. 10:30am–12:30pm.

camps.name: STEM Camp I
camps.description: Hands-on exploration of five senses, force and motion, astronomy, and life sciences. 1–3pm.

camps.name: Spanish for Travelers Camp
camps.description: Practical language skills and cultural immersion focusing on travel scenarios and Hispanic traditions. 1–3pm.

camps.name: Grade School Field Hockey Camp
camps.description: Fundamentals, tactical games, and scrimmages with ability-based grouping. 8:30–10:30am. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Camp EmpowerHER
camps.description: Leadership development rooted in self-discovery, community engagement, and Nerinx values. 9am–1pm.

camps.name: Game ON! Camp I
camps.description: Board games, card games, strategy challenges, and age-appropriate gaming emphasizing teamwork. 1–3pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Mock Trial: Order in the Camp!
camps.description: Legal education through courtroom roles, public speaking, and critical thinking challenges. 8:30–10:30am. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Tennis Camp II
camps.description: Advanced instruction for intermediate and experienced players. 10–11:30am at Webster Groves Recreation Center. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Adventures in Babysitting Camp
camps.description: Safety, first aid, and childcare techniques through hands-on role-playing. 10:30am–12:30pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Soccer Camp II
camps.description: Advanced drills and competitive scrimmages for experienced players. 10:30am–12:30pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Crafting with Heart Camp
camps.description: Macramé, paper quilling, and mixed-media techniques for personalized creations. 12:30–3:30pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Creative Writing Camp
camps.description: Character development and storytelling strategies to strengthen writing abilities. 1–3pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Dance Camp
camps.description: Pom, jazz, and hip-hop routines with technique refinement and performance opportunity. 8:30–10:30am. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Lacrosse Camp
camps.description: Cradling, passing, and scoring instruction with skill-building and competitive games. 1–3pm. Mon–Thu only.

camps.name: Volleyball Camp III
camps.description: Advanced-level training for experienced middle school athletes. 1–3pm. Mon–Thu only.
